title: narcissus pseudonarcissus var. montinus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus var. montinus, also known as the Welsh daffodil or mountain daffodil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Wales and other parts of the British Isles. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine. The medicinal properties of the Welsh daffodil have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, improving cognitive function, and potentially helping to manage symptoms of Alzheimer's disease. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
